weeks.weekly_summary: Foundation phase: 2 runs on preferred days (Monday, Wednesday). Light aerobic work with steady-state easy runs. Build consistency before introducing intensity.
weeks.coaching_notes: Recent Strava data shows ~10km/week at easy pace (5:50–6:10/km). This week establishes the pattern: 2 runs on Mon/Wed, rest on non-preferred days. No quality work yet — focus on building aerobic base and consistency. Recent volume is low, so ramping gradually to 16km/week (60% of time budget) prevents overstress.
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 2 adds volume to ~20km and introduces first quality session (tempo or fartlek) mid-week to build lactate threshold.

weeks.weekly_summary: Build volume to 20km. Introduce first quality session (tempo fartlek) on Wednesday to develop aerobic threshold. Continue easy run Monday for active recovery benefits.
weeks.coaching_notes: Volume increases from 16km to 20km (up 25%) — acceptable for moving from very light base to building block. First quality session placed mid-week (Wednesday) as a tempo/threshold effort. Monday stays easy to allow recovery from the weekend football. Pilates, Gym, and Football remain fixed.
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 3 continues to ~24km with longer easy run and second quality session (intervals) building VO2 max tolerance.

days.title: Tempo Fartlek 6km
days.description: Warm-up: 1.5km easy. Main: 3 × 1km at tempo pace (~5:20/km, comfortably hard) with 90s easy jog recovery. Cool-down: 1km easy. Target: sustained lactate-threshold work.
days.notes: First quality session. Aim for rhythm and control — not an all-out effort. Breathing should allow few words.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Recovery day. Allow adaptation from tempo work.

days.title: 🏋️ Gym
days.description: Fixed cross-training: Gym session.

days.title: ⚽ Football
days.description: Fixed cross-training: Football.

weeks.weekly_summary: Transition to higher volume (24km) and second quality session. Easy run Monday builds consistency; interval session Wednesday targets VO2 max. Begin slight progression toward aerobic development phase.
weeks.coaching_notes: Volume progression: 20 → 24km (20% increase, acceptable within polarized block). Two quality sessions per 4-week block means Week 3 holds the second hard effort: intervals (shorter, faster repeats targeting VO2 max). Pilates, Gym, Football remain fixed. This week signals gentle transition toward aerobic development (increased volume, varied intensity stimulus).
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 4 (final block week) maintains ~24km with one quality session (fartlek or hills) to consolidate gains before moving into Aerobic Development phase (weeks 5–8).

days.title: VO2 Max Intervals 6.5km
days.description: Warm-up: 1.5km easy. Main: 5 × 800m at VO2 max pace (~5:00/km, hard breathing, few words) with 2min easy jog recovery. Cool-down: 1km easy. Target: neuromuscular power and aerobic ceiling.
days.notes: Second quality session of block. Hold pace; aim for even splits on each rep.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Recovery. Allow neural and metabolic adaptation.

days.title: 🏋️ Gym

weeks.weekly_summary: Maintain block volume at 24km. Third quality session (fartlek) consolidates lactate-threshold and VO2 max stimulus. Final week of 4-week block; sets foundation for weeks 5–8 Aerobic Development phase.
weeks.coaching_notes: Week 4 holds volume steady (no taper yet — that begins week 13, 3 weeks before race). One additional quality session (fartlek mixing tempos and VO2 work) reinforces threshold and aerobic ceiling. This block transitions athlete from very light base (10km/week) to established 24km/week, preparing for Aerobic Development phase (weeks 5–8) which will add tempo runs and longer easy runs. Pilates, Gym, Football remain fixed.
weeks.next_week_preview: Block complete. Weeks 5–8 (Aerobic Development phase) will increase volume toward 30–32km/week with longer easy runs (10–12km) and sustained tempo sessions (6–8km @ threshold). Quality sessions remain 1–2/week.

days.title: Mixed Fartlek 7km
days.description: Warm-up: 1.5km easy. Main: 6 × (2min @ tempo ~5:20/km + 1min easy jog) + 2 × 1min fast (~4:50/km, VO2 effort) with 1.5min easy recovery. Cool-down: 1km easy. Target: blend lactate-threshold and VO2 max stimulus in varied session.
days.key_focus: Lactate threshold, VO2 max, mental resilience
days.notes: Final quality session of block. Focus on holding form through varied pace changes.

days.description: Recovery. Block completion week.

coaching_overview:
### 4-Week Base Building Block (Weeks 1–4, 15 weeks to race)

**Volume Progression:** 16 → 20 → 24 → 24 km/week (80% easy, 20% hard via 3 quality sessions across block).

**Polarized Training Applied:**
- **Monday runs:** Steady easy aerobic (Zone 2, 6:15/km). Active recovery and consistency building.
- **Wednesday workouts:** Quality sessions (Weeks 2–4: Tempo Fartlek → VO2 Max Intervals → Mixed Fartlek). Develops lactate threshold and aerobic ceiling.
- **Fixed cross-training:** Pilates (Tue, light), Gym (Fri, moderate), Football (Sat, high intensity) integrated within time budget.
- **Rest/Recovery:** Thursday and Sunday reserved for passive recovery, ensuring adaptation.

**Key Principles:**
1. **Progressive overload:** Volume increases 20–25% week-to-week (acceptable range); intensity stimulus varied to prevent monotony and plateau.
2. **Polarized stimulus:** Hard workouts (intervals, tempo, fartlek) kept to ~20% effort; 80% easy/recovery work dominates.
3. **Schedule adherence:** All runs placed on preferred days (Monday, Wednesday); cross-training fixed commitments respected.
4. **Half-marathon preparation:** With 15 weeks to race, this block establishes aerobic base and work capacity. Next phase (weeks 5–8, Aerobic Development) will increase volume to 28–32 km/week with longer easy runs (10–12km) and sustained tempo/threshold work, moving athlete toward peak fitness window (weeks 9–12).

**Athlete Readiness:**
- Recent Strava: ~10km/week at easy pace (5:50–6:10/km). Block ramps to 24km by week 4 while maintaining easy pace discipline and building tolerance to structured intensity.
- No overtraining flags detected; consistent progression with adequate recovery.
- Time budget (4.0 hrs/week) accommodated: typical week ≈ 3.5–4.0 hrs running + cross-training.

**Next Steps (Week 5+):**
Begin Aerobic Development phase. Extend Monday easy runs to 10–12km, introduce Wednesday tempo sessions (6–8km sustained @ lactate threshold), and maintain Friday/Saturday cross-training. Continue polarized 80/20 model.

No quality work yet — focus on building aerobic base and consistency. Recent volume is low, so ramping gradually to 16km/week (60% of time budget) prevents overstress.
